title: Finish compaction, archive-boundary trust continuity, and operator diagnostics
summary: Complete the operational side of shared coordination refs so compaction preserves trust semantics, archive boundaries stay intelligible, and operators can see enough history and health to debug live systems.

acceptance.label: Compaction preserves explicit trust continuity or archive-boundary semantics instead of discarding verification history implicitly.
Operator surfaces expose current head, last verified manifest, last successful publish, retry counts, and compaction health.
Bounded recent-history and archive/export behavior are explicit, documented, and covered by tests.
